bull.b: Material positive FCF and operating cash flow provide durable internal funding for R&D, working capital and capacity investments, reducing reliance on external financing. Even with a ~15% FCF decline versus prior period, sustained positive cash conversion supports multi-quarter execution and strategic optionality.

Broad design wins across 800G/1.6T and substantial backlog signal durable demand and multi-quarter revenue visibility. Hyperscaler engagements and ramp plans underpin higher‑margin product mix and sustained revenue growth potential in infrastructure markets over the coming 2–6 months.

bull.t: LoRa Platform And IoT Systems Momentum
bull.b: Stewardship of LoRa, improving LoRa revenue and positive reception for new AirLink routers create platform-driven recurring and attach-rate opportunities. Combined hardware, connectivity services (post‑Sierra Wireless) and ecosystem adoption support more predictable, sticky revenue streams over multiple quarters.

bear.t: Top-line Volatility And GAAP Net Losses
bear.b: A shrinking TTM top line and ongoing GAAP net losses limit free distributions to shareholders and make earnings recovery dependent on sustained revenue improvement. Persistent top-line pressure increases execution risk for margin targets and constrains return of capital until profitability stabilizes.

Severe GaN supply constraints can cap near-term revenue growth and force heavy incremental capital spending and long lead‑time clean-room expansion. If capacity lags demand, the company may lose share, delay customer ramps, and face higher per‑unit costs that compress margins over several quarters.

Acquisition-related ramp and integration costs are depressing gross margins in signal integrity. These one-time and ramp-phase expenses can persist across quarters until yields and throughput normalize, reducing near-term margin sustainability and delaying return on invested capital from the acquisition.
